mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS reflects a Fed tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from 0.4% in 2016 to 1.41% by Jan 2018, marking an active hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and forward 60-day SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year walk-forward window, or returns are positive in >55% of months with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 4.0%, the labor market is historically tight enough to trigger wage-inflation expectations among investors, raising fears of accelerated Fed tightening and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in Jan 2018, near multi-decade lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE readings below 4.0% are not significantly more negative than the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical tight-labor episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint occurrence of rising CPIAUCSL (accelerating inflation) and rising FEDFUNDS (active tightening) signals an inflation-surprise regime in which bond yields spike unexpectedly, triggering a rapid equity de-risking shock and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week, consistent with the volatility dynamics observed in early February 2018.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change accelerates while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.245 with FEDFUNDS at 1.41% ahead of the Feb 2018 volatility spike
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation surprise
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in periods where both CPIAUCSL acceleration and FEDFUNDS increases co-occur, across at least three historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals weakening forward-looking consumer demand and construction activity, which historically precedes broader economic slowdown and depresses S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1192k units in Dec 2017, a cycle-sensitive leading demand indicator
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month HOUST decline magnitude and forward 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, which is positively priced into equities and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 in Jan 2018, trending upward from prior months
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following positive INDPRO momentum are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS month-over-month growth above 0.5% signals robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,003 million dollars in Jan 2018, reflecting strong consumer demand post tax-reform
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following RSAFS growth above 0.5% are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05) across at least three historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS 3-month average growth below its trailing 12-month trend signals weakening labor demand and future consumption capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth falls more than 20% below its trailing 12-month average growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,810 thousand persons in Jan 2018, near cycle highs
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth deceleration and forward 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 across any rolling 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with an already tight labor market, a slowdown in GDPC1 quarterly growth combined with continued FEDFUNDS increases signals that monetary tightening is beginning to bite real growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; this mechanism is muted or reversed in early-cycle recovery regimes where tightening reflects confidence rather than restraint.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,272.468 billion in Q3 2017 with FEDFUNDS rising to 1.41% amid a maturing expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint GDPC1 growth deceleration and FEDFUNDS increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the relationship reverses sign in more than half of late-cycle episodes tested.
